mnemonics: Most Efficient Algorithm Provides Comparisons Quickly
Counting Helps To Organize Data Efficiently
keyPoints: Comparison Counting Sort is a stable sorting algorithm
It uses a count array to store the number of occurrences of each element
The time complexity is O(n) for best case and O(n^2) for average and worst case
It is a comparison-based sorting algorithm and uses more space
The algorithm works by counting the number of occurrences of each element in the array
It then uses these counts to construct the sorted array
